description: Take successful app and adapt for underserved demographic
source_app_criteria: Trending on app stores (use appkittie). Good reviews. Simple core feature.
target_niche_criteria: Underserved segment: women, specific religion, profession, age group, location
execution_steps: 1. Find trending app 2. Research target niche demand 3. Adapt UI/copy/features 4. Build MVP 5. Test with niche community
examples: Meditation app → Christian meditation. Habit tracker → Muslim prayer tracker. Fitness app → Women's fitness.
notes: Greg Isenberg framework. Build community first.

description: Find terminated YouTube channels rebuild their content strategy on new platforms
source_app_criteria: Terminated channels with high subs/views (algrow.online). Content that can be safely recreated.
target_niche_criteria: Same audience but legal content approach. Avoid whatever caused termination.
execution_steps: 1. Monitor algrow.online 2. Analyze terminated channel content 3. Identify safe spin 4. Rebuild on TikTok/YT Shorts 5. Capture abandoned audience
examples: Terminated finance channel → compliant investing tips. Terminated health channel → general wellness.
notes: Must avoid whatever caused original termination.

description: Find apps with bad reviews solve specific complaints
source_app_criteria: 1-2 star reviews on App Store/Play Store. Specific repeatable complaints.
target_niche_criteria: Users frustrated with specific feature or UX issue.
execution_steps: 1. Search G2/Capterra/App Store for competitor 2. Read 1-2 star reviews 3. Identify pattern complaints 4. Build solution 5. Target those users
tools_needed: G2, Capterra, App Store, Play Store
examples: CRM with bad mobile app → build mobile-first version. Project tool with confusing UI → build simpler version.
notes: Low risk. Users already want solution.

description: Take US-successful app adapt for specific country/language
source_app_criteria: US app not available in target country. Or available but not localized well.
target_niche_criteria: Country with demand but no good local option.
execution_steps: 1. Find US apps with global potential 2. Research target country demand 3. Localize copy/features/payments 4. Launch in target market
tools_needed: App Store (filter by country), translation services
examples: US productivity app → French version. US faith app → Spanish-speaking markets.

description: Take successful web tool and make mobile app version
source_app_criteria: Popular web tool with no mobile app. Users complaining about mobile experience.
target_niche_criteria: Mobile-first users who want the tool on the go.
execution_steps: 1. Find popular web tools 2. Check if mobile app exists 3. Build mobile wrapper or native app 4. Market to existing user base
tools_needed: React Native, Flutter, or web wrapper tools
examples: Web-based calculator → mobile app. Web dashboard → mobile companion.
notes: Can be simple wrapper or full native.

description: Take horizontal SaaS and shrink to specific vertical
source_app_criteria: General purpose tool (CRM, project mgmt, etc). Works but not optimized for specific industry.
target_niche_criteria: Specific industry with unique needs: churches, gyms, restaurants, etc.
execution_steps: 1. Identify horizontal tool with wide use 2. Pick vertical with specific needs 3. Build simplified version 4. Add vertical-specific features 5. Price for vertical
examples: General CRM → Church CRM. General scheduling → Gym scheduling. General invoicing → Contractor invoicing.

description: Build free alternative to popular paid tool
source_app_criteria: Paid tool ($20-100/mo) with features that can be replicated. Users complaining about price.
execution_steps: 1. Find paid tools with price complaints 2. Identify core features needed 3. Build free or cheaper version 4. Monetize with premium tier or ads
tools_needed: Standard dev tools, open source libs
examples: Paid AI writing tool → free version with limits. Paid SEO tool → free basic version.
notes: Compete on price for bottom of market.

description: Aggregate multiple single-purpose apps into one
source_app_criteria: Users using 5+ apps for related tasks. Switching cost high.
target_niche_criteria: People who want all-in-one solution for a workflow.
execution_steps: 1. Identify related single-purpose apps 2. Map common user workflows 3. Build unified solution 4. Import from existing tools
examples: Multiple content tools → unified content OS. Multiple fitness trackers → unified health dashboard.
notes: Higher effort. Strong retention if executed well.

description: Build simple UI around powerful API
source_app_criteria: Complex API with no good UI. Developers use it but non-technical can't.
target_niche_criteria: Non-technical users who want API functionality.
execution_steps: 1. Find powerful APIs (OpenAI, etc) 2. Identify use case for non-technical users 3. Build simple UI wrapper 4. Charge for convenience
examples: GPT API → simple writing tool. Image API → one-click generator.
notes: Fast to build. Compete on UX.

description: Build community before product then build what they want
source_app_criteria: Audience already engaged in a topic. Willing to pay for solutions.
target_niche_criteria: Community with clear pain points and willingness to pay.
execution_steps: 1. Build audience (X, newsletter, Discord) 2. Engage and learn pain points 3. Pre-sell solution 4. Build based on demand 5. Launch to warm audience
examples: Faith community → faith app. Fitness community → fitness tracking tool.
notes: @purpdevvv framework. Lowest risk if executed well.

description: Find winning digital products via FB Ads Library search
source_app_criteria: Products actively paying for ads = making money. Search keywords: Ebook, Digital Download, PDF, Printable, Template, Guide, Course, Planner.
target_niche_criteria: Same keywords adapted to your niche. Info products, digital downloads.
execution_steps: 1. Go to FB Ads Library 2. Search product keywords 3. Filter by active ads 4. Analyze landing pages 5. Create better version for niche
tools_needed: Facebook Ads Library (free), landing page builder
examples: Find winning fitness ebook → create faith version. Find template → adapt for profession.
notes: @xivy0k method. If they're paying for ads, they're making money.

description: Build niche-specific screen time blocker apps
source_app_criteria: Trending: Opal $600k/mo, BePresent $300k/mo, Brainrot $100k/mo. Core feature: block phone until X.
target_niche_criteria: Niche unlock conditions: Pray, Walk, Workout, Complete task, Read scripture.
execution_steps: 1. Study Opal/BePresent mechanics 2. Pick niche unlock condition 3. Build with Flutter/React Native 4. Hard paywall from day 1 5. Content marketing on TikTok
notes: @Jahjiren insight. Pattern recognition: blockers printing. Niche = differentiation.

description: Add Harvard/medical citations + visual goal timelines + bright paywall
source_app_criteria: Apps with $350k+ MRR using: 1) Authority citations 2) Visualized outcomes 3) Bright conversion CTAs.
target_niche_criteria: Health, fitness, productivity apps where trust matters.
execution_steps: 1. Add legitimate source citations (Harvard, Mayo, etc) 2. Build visual goal timeline showing user progress 3. Design paywall with bright colors + 'goals 4.2x faster' style copy
tools_needed: Standard mobile stack, Canva for visuals
examples: Fitness app citing Harvard studies. Meditation app with progress timeline. Habit tracker with achievement visuals.
notes: @matteo_spada breakdown. Trust signals + outcome visualization = conversion.

description: Build niche-specific UI around AI APIs (OpenAI/Anthropic/Google)
source_app_criteria: Successful AI wrappers: Jasper $80M ARR, Copy.ai $50M ARR. API does heavy lifting, you add UX + workflow.
target_niche_criteria: Niche verticals: faith content, fitness plans, cold email, app listings. Non-technical users who want AI power.
execution_steps: 1. Pick specific niche (not generic) 2. Build landing page + waitlist 3. MVP with one core feature 4. Use cheapest model that works 5. Subscription or credit pricing 6. Content marketing to niche
examples: SermonGen (faith), ColdMailMaxx (B2B), FitCoach AI (fitness), AppDescribe (developers). Jasper, Copy.ai, Writesonic.
notes: API is commodity. Niche focus + workflow + GTM = the product. See MONEY_METHODS/AI_WRAPPER/ for full guide.
